methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Jimmy Energy is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Antoine Guyot and Mathilde Grivet, having raised €80M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private nuclear energy firms, including Fuse Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Oklo.

The peer-median method is not applicable here. The nuclear energy sector lacks sufficient same-stage comparables to support a reliable triangulation: only 1 same-stage peer and 1 adjacent-stage peer were identified within the narrow peer set, falling short of the 3-datapoint minimum threshold required for statistical validity. While 8 private peers exist in the nuclear energy sector broadly, none cluster tightly enough around Jimmy Energy's stage and valuation tier to produce a defensible median benchmark. An all-sector median would introduce excessive noise and obscure sector-specific dynamics.

The absence of a quantified triangulated range reflects this constraint. Valuation support for Jimmy Energy at Series A would require either: (1) direct comparable transaction data from the identified peer set (Fuse Energy, Kairos Power, X-Energy, TerraPower), or (2) expansion to broader frontier-energy analogues, each introducing different methodological trade-offs. Key uncertainties center on the relative maturity and technology differentiation of peers, capital intensity profiles, and path-to-revenue timelines within the fragmented advanced nuclear market.
